project.title: Palantir Math Problem 15 Motion Lecture Pilot

requirements.meaning: Build a Hyperframes video editing project, define reusable multi-project folders, and specify how scenes are edited before rendering.
If the user provides MP4 narration, locally recognize speech timing with Whisper so the video knows what was said when.

visualContext.rule: The SRT controls narration order. Sequencer refs only attach visual context for formulas, conditions, and graph expressions.

workflow.editingModel: script-first scene map, then Hyperframes composition
workflow.captionStrategy: Use final SRT directly; use repo-local whisper.cpp only for MP4-only timing or validation.

bottlenecks.reason: The final SRT is conversational, so scene boundaries must be semantic rather than raw frame-index based.
bottlenecks.nextAction: Review generated scene boundaries against the intended lecture rhythm.

bottlenecks.reason: Sequencer active refs say which formulas and graphs are meaningful, but they do not carry final video timestamps.
bottlenecks.nextAction: Attach refs to scene spans before generating composition HTML.

bottlenecks.reason: The repo-local large-v3 Whisper model can align MP4 narration, but this fixture already has an SRT.
bottlenecks.nextAction: Use Whisper as a validation path for this fixture and as the default path for MP4-only projects.

sceneMap.label: Decompose the problem conditions
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Open from the final edited lecture script and establish D1-D2 as the first visual context.
motionDirectives: Reveal the cubic-function condition before showing the integral definition.
Use slow condition-card staging rather than dense formula motion.
bottleneckNotes: The SRT is newer than the source math JSON, so transcript order wins when frame order differs.

sceneMap.label: Translate g(x) into derivative/root-count language
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Move from the integral definition to g'(x)=0 and the meaning of four distinct roots.
motionDirectives: Animate |f(x)| and |x| as two comparable curves.
Keep the derivative/root-count interpretation visible as a side anchor.
bottleneckNotes: Whisper or SRT timing alone cannot know which condition card should be active; Sequencer refs supply that context.

sceneMap.label: First root-count and extrema interpretation
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Connect roots of g'(x) with crossings and sign changes near x=2 and x=6.
sceneMap.captionPolicy: side caption when graph markers are dense
motionDirectives: Use crossing markers at x=2 and x=6.
Distinguish contact from sign-changing intersection with color and motion direction.
bottleneckNotes: The final script speaks in exploratory order; a rigid frame-by-frame replay would feel late here.

sceneMap.label: Revisit g'(x) with |x| fixed
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Use |x| as the fixed comparison curve and treat f(x) as the adjustable object.
motionDirectives: Pin |x| visually while moving or reshaping f(x).
Avoid decorative motion; the viewer must read which graph is fixed.
bottleneckNotes: This is the first major semantic split from raw captions: the visual must express fixed-vs-adjustable roles.

sceneMap.label: Interpret extrema as sign-changing crossings
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Make D3 and D4 active together: four roots plus extrema at 2 and 6 require sign-change crossings.
sceneMap.captionPolicy: lower-third or side caption, never over graph roots
motionDirectives: Show four possible root slots before narrowing to valid crossings.
Use pulse markers only at sign-changing roots.
bottleneckNotes: Caption grouping should not hide the graph; this scene needs lower-third captions or side captions.

sceneMap.label: Avoid absolute-value folding traps
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Explain why the work should compare f(x) against plus/minus x instead of over-folding the graph.
sceneMap.captionPolicy: short caption groups because this scene needs internal micro-beats
motionDirectives: Introduce y=x and y=-x as two reference lines.
Fade out invalid folded interpretations rather than deleting them abruptly.
bottleneckNotes: This scene is the longest early hold; it needs micro-beats inside one semantic scene.

sceneMap.label: Construct the four-root shape
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Build the candidate shape that can satisfy four roots and the extrema information.
motionDirectives: Accumulate root markers in sequence.
Keep invalid candidates ghosted so the narrowing logic remains visible.
bottleneckNotes: Scene overlaps prior frame refs by design; semantic continuity is more important than strict frame partitioning.

sceneMap.label: Apply f(6) x g(2) < 0
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Use D5 as the decisive condition and split cases by signs of f(6) and g(2).
sceneMap.captionPolicy: right-side caption while sign tiles animate
motionDirectives: Show f(6) and g(2) as separate sign tiles.
Animate rejected sign cases with a short strike-through.
bottleneckNotes: The script discusses case selection conversationally; the plan must turn that into explicit visual branches.

sceneMap.label: Explain g(2) as signed area
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Translate g(2)<0 into accumulated signed area between |f(t)| and |t|.
sceneMap.captionPolicy: minimal captions; reserve canvas for area shading
motionDirectives: Shade negative and positive area regions separately.
Use area accumulation motion rather than another formula reveal.
bottleneckNotes: This is the highest-risk visual bottleneck: captions must align to area explanation, not just the spoken words.

sceneMap.label: Check the final shape against all root-count conditions
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Verify the constructed shape against the count and crossing requirements.
motionDirectives: Replay final candidate with root-count badges.
Keep all D1-D5 condition badges in a compact checklist.
bottleneckNotes: A checklist gives recovery when the narration references earlier conditions without restating them fully.

sceneMap.label: Leave the algebraic calculation as the next step
sceneMap.semanticGoal: Close by naming the three equations that determine P, Q, R and defer the arithmetic.
motionDirectives: Switch from graph space to a three-equation summary.
End on a clean calculation handoff rather than a solved-number reveal.
bottleneckNotes: The final video should not invent a solution beyond the user's final lecture script.
